sourceFiles.name: thermal-system

results.name: 04_redundancy
results.rawText:
Model Characterisation
- System purpose: A thermal protection/control model defining when a system should activate cooling and when it should enter emergency shutdown based on temperature thresholds.
- Operational domain: Industrial/embedded control or thermal management.
- Model scope: Small fragment containing two requirement definitions and one part definition with threshold attributes.
- Known scope limitations: No behavioural model, no state machines, no decomposition hierarchy, no interfaces, no relationships (`satisfy`, `allocate`, `bind`, etc.), and no verification cases are present. Checks requiring those elements are assessed only for what is explicitly present; absent categories are skipped where appropriate rather than treated as defects.

CHECK PASSED: 1. Duplicate Requirements  -  The two requirements were examined for identical or near-identical wording and they express different constraints: emergency shutdown at 80°C versus cooling fan activation at 75°C.

CHECK PASSED: 2. Duplicate Block or Part Definitions  -  Only one `part def` (`thermal_system::CoolingSystem`) is present, so there is no duplicated block, part, item, or interface definition to compare.

CHECK PASSED: 3. Redundant Relationships  -  No `satisfy`, `allocate`, `connect`, `bind`, or specialization relationships are present, so no duplicated relationships exist in this model fragment.

CHECK PASSED: 4. Redundant Behaviour Models  -  No state machines, action definitions, sequences, or message exchange models are present, so there is no behavioural duplication to assess in this fragment.

CHECK PASSED: 5. Redundant Attributes & Constraints  -  The attributes `temperatureC`, `emergencyShutdownThresholdC`, and `coolingFanStartThresholdC` are distinct and not repeated elsewhere as duplicate attributes, derived copies, or repeated constraint applications.

CHECK PASSED: 6. Redundant Decomposition  -  The model contains no decomposition hierarchy, intermediate pass-through blocks, empty packages, or single-step action decompositions; the single package contains substantive content.

CHECK PASSED: 7. Unintentional vs. Intentional Redundancy  -  No duplicated structural or requirement elements were found, so there is no apparent redundancy requiring justification as intentional design redundancy.

CHECK PASSED: 8. States that are never activated  -  Although this check concerns redundant unreachable behaviour, the stated thresholds are not contradictory in the provided fragment: cooling fan activation at 75°C can occur before emergency shutdown at 80°C, and no behaviour model is present showing unreachable states.

| ID | Score | Confidence | One-line description |
|---|---:|---|---|
| None | 1.0 | HIGH | No redundancy issues identified in the provided model fragment |

Overall Assessment Score: 1.0  
Model Status: Good - minor improvements only
